channel.title: Darkhorse Press - Top Story
channel.description: Latest Top Story posts from Darkhorse Press.

item.title: Waveland Police Seek Help Identifying Suspect in Vehicle Burglaries, Stolen Car - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Therese Apel
item.description:
WAVELAND — Police in Waveland are asking for the public’s help identifying a person caught on camera who investigators say is connected to several vehicle burglaries and the theft of a vehicle earlier this month.

According to the Waveland Police Department, the incidents occurred Tuesday, March 3, 2026, when the suspect allegedly broke into multiple vehicles and stole a vehicle from a Ford dealership in Waveland.

Authorities released surveillance images of the individual and are asking anyone who recognizes the person to come forward.

Police said anyone with information about the suspect or the crimes is urged to contact Crime Stoppers by downloading the P3 Tips app or by calling **TIPS.

Officials noted that in order to qualify for any potential reward, information must be submitted through Crime Stoppers.

The investigation remains ongoing.

item.title: Tuesday Weather: Highs in the lower 80s, rainy/storms ahead - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Mary Apel
item.description:
Severe storms capable of damaging winds, hail up to golf ball size, and tornadoes will be possible Wednesday afternoon and evening.

Today we will see cloudy skies and highs in the lower 80s across the region.

item.title: Terry police investigating overturned truck on Highway 473 - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Therese Apel
item.description:
TERRY — Terry Police officers responded at 5:17 p.m. to a one-vehicle accident involving a 2024 Freightliner on State Highway 473 near Gaines Drive.

According to Terry Police Chief Anthony Moore, the truck overturned into a ditch in front of a cemetery at that location. The vehicle sustained heavy damage.

Moore said the two occupants of the truck were not injured.

The accident remains under investigation by the Terry Police Department.

The accident remains under investigation by the Terry Police Department.

item.title: Mother, daughter die in Montgomery County house fire - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
KILMICHAEL, Miss. — According to WTVA, a mother and daughter died in a house fire in Montgomery County on Friday morning.

Montgomery County Sheriff Jeff Tompkins identified the victims as Mary Pittman, 68, and Consietta Pittman, 42.

Tompkins said Mary Pittman was wheelchair-bound and bed-bound, and her daughter served as her caregiver.

Officials said the cause of the fire remains under investigation, but foul play is not suspected, according to WTVA.

item.title: Lucedale man killed in motorcycle crash in Greene County - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
GREENE COUNTY – A Lucedale man was killed Monday afternoon in a motorcycle crash in Greene County, according to the Mississippi Highway Patrol.

The crash happened March 9 at approximately 3:56 p.m. on U.S. Highway 63 near Pleasant Hill Road.

Authorities said a 2011 Harley-Davidson motorcycle driven by Ross M. Eubanks, 56, of Lucedale, was traveling south on U.S. 63 when it left the roadway and struck the tree line.

Eubanks sustained fatal injuries in the crash, officials said.

The Mississippi Highway Patrol is continuing to investigate.

item.title: Lake Man Charged With Child Enticement, Human Trafficking After Cleveland Investigation - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Therese Apel
item.description:
CLEVELAND — A Lake man was arrested following a monthlong investigation and charged with enticement of a child to produce sexually explicit material and human trafficking, according to the Cleveland Police Department.

Bailey Jones, 30, was arrested last week and charged with one count of enticement of a child to depict sexually explicit material and one count of human trafficking, police said.

Authorities also executed a search warrant at Jones’ home during the investigation with assistance from multiple state and local agencies.

Jones was transported to Bolivar County, where he appeared before Cleveland Municipal Court Judge Warren Bell for his initial court appearance. Bell set Jones’ bond at $750,000 on each charge, according to police.

Jones is currently being held at the Bolivar County Jail.

The search warrant and investigation involved assistance from several agencies, including the Mississippi Bureau of Investigation Special Victims Unit, the Mississippi Attorney General’s Office, the Mississippi Department of Public Safety Crime Scene Unit, the Mississippi Highway Patrol, the Mississippi Department of Corrections K-9 Unit and the Scott County Sheriff’s Department.

Cleveland Police Chief Dudley Tribble thanked investigators and the assisting agencies for their work on the case.

Authorities noted that Jones is presumed innocent unless proven guilty in court.

item.title: Inmates at Quitman Community Work Center earn forklift certification - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
QUITMAN COUNTY – Twenty-five participants at the Quitman Community Work Center recently completed forklift training through a partnership between the Mississippi Department of Corrections and Coahoma Community College.

The training program concluded Friday, March 6, and provided participants with hands-on instruction designed to prepare them for employment opportunities after reentry into the community.

Officials said the certification gives participants industry-recognized skills while also helping build confidence and readiness to enter the workforce.

The Mississippi Department of Corrections said programs like the forklift training are part of broader efforts to reduce recidivism and create pathways to meaningful employment for individuals preparing to return to society.

MDOC officials also credited community partnerships, including Coahoma Community College, for helping provide training opportunities that support rehabilitation and workforce development.

item.title: George County man arrested on child exploitation charges - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
LUCEDALE – A Lucedale man has been arrested on multiple charges of touching a child for lustful purposes, according to the George County Sheriff’s Office.

Investigators arrested Matthew Bradshaw, 43, on March 9.

Bradshaw was charged with three counts of touching a child for lustful purposes. Authorities also said he had two outstanding warrants through George County Justice Court for guilty in absentia.

The charges stem from an investigation that began after the sheriff’s office received a report from Child Protection Services. Investigators later developed information that led to Bradshaw’s arrest, officials said.

Bradshaw was given a $60,000 commercial bond on each of the three child exploitation charges, for a total bond of $180,000.

Sheriff Mitchell Mixon said the sheriff’s office appreciates the work of Child Protection Services in cases involving the safety and well-being of children.

All defendants are presumed innocent until proven guilty in a court of law.

item.title: Forrest County authorities searching for missing man who may be experiencing medical emergency - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
FORREST COUNTY – The Forrest County Sheriff’s Office is asking for the public’s help locating a missing man who may be experiencing a medical emergency.

Authorities said Chad Moseley, 56, was last seen March 5 at about 7 p.m. at the Dollar General on Evelyn Gandy Parkway.

Investigators believe Moseley may be without his medication, raising concerns for his safety.

Anyone who sees Moseley or has information about his whereabouts is asked to contact the Forrest County Sheriff’s Office at 601-544-7800.

item.title: Clarkco State Park to Reopen March 19 With Renovated Cabins, Campgrounds - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Therese Apel
item.description:
QUITMAN — Clarkco State Park in Quitman will reopen March 19 following renovations that include updated RV campsites, newly renovated cabins and improvements to other park facilities, officials with the Mississippi Department of Wildlife, Fisheries and Parks announced.

The park will celebrate its grand reopening with a public event beginning at 8 a.m. March 19 with a social gathering at Pavilion Two, followed by a special program. Guests will also have the opportunity to tour the park’s newly renovated cabins and campground after the program.

Clarkco State Park now includes 43 updated campsites designed for RV camping, each equipped with full hookups including water, electricity and sewer, along with a picnic table and grill, according to MDWFP.

Officials say the upgrades are intended to make the park a more comfortable destination for visitors looking to enjoy outdoor recreation in Mississippi.

The park also features six newly renovated cabins, each outfitted with updated amenities including stainless steel kitchen appliances such as a refrigerator, stove, microwave and coffee station. The cabins also include renovated dining areas with solid-surface countertops, new living room furniture with a flatscreen satellite television, updated bedroom furniture and bathrooms with walk-in fiberglass showers.

Each cabin also features new outdoor spaces including a front porch with a picnic table, fire pit and grill, as well as a screened-in back porch with outdoor seating.

Additional renovations at the park include updates to the lodge and bathhouse, according to the department.

Reservations for cabins and campsites are now open and can be made online through the Mississippi Department of Wildlife, Fisheries and Parks reservation system.

Visitors to Clarkco State Park can also take part in a variety of recreational activities including fishing, boating and hiking, along with access to a day-use area featuring a playground, nature trail, disc golf course and tennis courts.

Park entry costs $2 per person, and children age 5 and younger are admitted free.

item.title: Child found dead after vehicle crashes into creek off Natchez Trace Parkway - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
LEE COUNTY – A female child was found dead inside a vehicle Tuesday morning after it crashed off the Natchez Trace Parkway and into a creek in Lee County, according to WTVA.

The Lee County coroner confirmed the child’s death. First responders were also searching for another missing person, WTVA reported.

According to Tupelo Fire Chief Thomas Walker, the crash happened around 8:30 p.m. Monday during heavy rain and flash flooding in the area.

Authorities said a witness saw the vehicle leave the parkway and enter the creek, then immediately called 911.

Police and fire crews searched the area for hours but were initially unable to locate the vehicle. Investigators later used a drone to search the area and found the car in the creek between Palmetto Road and Graham Drive.

Officers located the female child inside the vehicle. She was pronounced dead at the scene.

WTVA reported that members of Mississippi Task Force 1 from Monroe and Lee counties, the Tupelo Fire Department, Tupelo Police Department, Lee County Sheriff’s Office and Mississippi Department of Wildlife, Fisheries and Parks were involved in the search for the other missing person.

Officials have not yet released the child’s identity. The crash remains under investigation.

item.title: Biloxi man sentenced to 10 years in federal prison for child sexual abuse material charge - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
BILOXI – Mississippi Attorney General Lynn Fitch announced the sentencing of a Biloxi man on a child sexual abuse material charge following a joint investigation by state and federal authorities.

Terrance Allan Green, 46, of Biloxi, was sentenced on one count of possession of child sexual abuse material, according to the attorney general’s office.

The case was investigated by the Mississippi Attorney General’s Office and the Federal Bureau of Investigation, with assistance from the Biloxi Police Department. It was prosecuted by the U.S. Attorney’s Office for the Southern District of Mississippi.

“Predators who seek to exploit children in Mississippi will not go unpunished, and my office is committed to holding these individuals accountable,” Fitch said in a statement. “I am grateful to our partners at the FBI and the U.S. Attorney’s Office for their work to secure this sentence. Together, we are making Mississippi a safer place for everyone.”

On March 6, U.S. District Court Chief Judge Sul Ozerden sentenced Green to 10 years in the custody of the Federal Bureau of Prisons, followed by lifetime supervised release.

Green was also ordered to pay $15,000 in restitution and $3,000 in fees. Officials said he is already registered as a sex offender.

According to the attorney general’s office, Green’s arrest and sentencing stemmed from the same 2025 undercover operation initiated by the office that also led to the sentencing of Steven Dessino last month.

item.title: Bank of Commerce Donates $250,000 to Six North Mississippi Organizations Through Mississippi Children’s Promise Act - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Special to Darkhorse Press
item.description:
GREENWOOD – Bank of Commerce is proud to announce a total donation of $250,000 to six organizations across North Mississippi through the Mississippi Children’s Promise Act, supporting programs that provide care, resources, and critical support for children and families throughout the region.

The Children’s Promise Act, passed by the Mississippi Legislature in 2019, provides a dollar-for-dollar tax credit to individuals and companies that donate to qualified charitable organizations serving children in need.

Through this initiative, Bank of Commerce provided support to the following organizations:

“These organizations provide essential services and compassionate care for children and families across our communities,” said Bryan Thornhill, President and CEO of Bank of Commerce. “We are honored to support their work and invest in organizations that make a meaningful difference in the lives of Mississippi families.”

“At Bank of Commerce, our goal has always been to support the places where we live and work,” said Samantha Pigott, Vice President of Deposit & Treasury Solutions at Bank of Commerce. “We are proud to stand alongside these organizations and the important work they do every day.”

The donation will help these organizations continue providing critical services including family support programs, counseling, housing assistance, and resources that strengthen children and families across the region.

Bank of Commerce has a long-standing commitment to investing in the communities it serves. Through initiatives like the Children’s Promise Act, the bank continues to partner with local organizations to help strengthen Mississippi families and build a stronger future for the region.

item.title: Pearl man arrested in ongoing Rankin County narcotics investigation - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
PEARL – Law enforcement officials have made another arrest as part of an ongoing narcotics investigation in Rankin County, according to the Rankin County Sheriff’s Department.

On March 9, investigators with the Rankin County Sheriff’s Department and the Pearl Police Department, along with agents from multiple federal agencies, executed a search warrant and an arrest warrant at a home on Bruin Avenue in Pearl.

Authorities said the search warrant was obtained following an extensive investigation into drug activity in the area.

Steven Richard Hoffland was identified during the investigation as allegedly receiving large quantities of narcotics and selling the drugs.

Hoffland was arrested and charged with possession of a controlled substance, trafficking a controlled substance and sale of a controlled substance.

He was booked into the Rankin County Jail, and his bond has been set at $900,000.

Officials said Hoffland will appear before Rankin County Court Judge David Morrow for his initial court appearance.

Investigators said the broader investigation into drug trafficking in the metro area is ongoing and additional arrests are expected.

Rankin County Sheriff Bryan Bailey thanked Pearl Police Chief Nick McClendon, officers with the Pearl Police Department and other agencies involved for their cooperation in the investigation.

item.title: Tupelo man charged with aggravated trafficking of cocaine after drug investigation - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
TUPELO – A Tupelo man has been arrested on an aggravated trafficking of cocaine charge following a traffic stop and search warrant, according to the North Mississippi Narcotics Unit.

Authorities said agents made a traffic stop March 5 on a black Ford F-150 on South Eason Boulevard in Tupelo. The driver was identified as Anthony Dixon.

During the stop, agents developed probable cause to search the vehicle, which resulted in the discovery of a felony amount of suspected cocaine and marijuana.

Investigators later executed a search warrant at Dixon’s home on Pierce Street in Tupelo. During that search, agents said they found more than 1 1/2 pounds of suspected cocaine, 1 pound of suspected marijuana and more than $175,000 in cash.

Anthony Christopher Dixon, 46, of Tupelo, was arrested and charged with aggravated trafficking of cocaine.

Officials said Dixon was ordered held without bond by Tupelo Municipal Court Judge Willie Allen.

The investigation remains ongoing, and additional charges may be pending.

item.title: Three juveniles charged as adults in Vicksburg shooting investigation - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
VICKSBURG – Three juveniles have been charged as adults in connection with a shooting that occurred March 5 in Vicksburg, according to the Vicksburg Police Department.

Police said the shooting happened at the intersection of Randolph Street and Locust Street. No injuries were reported.

Following an investigation, officers identified three juvenile suspects who were taken into custody. The suspects appeared in court March 6, where they were adjudicated and charged as adults.

Authorities identified the suspects as Zion Pierce, 16, and Brian Bell, 16, both charged with aggravated assault. Each received a $600,000 bond.

Kaiden Knight, 15, was charged with aggravated assault and shooting into a motor vehicle. His bond was set at $550,000.

Police said the investigation remains active and additional information will be released as it becomes available.

Anyone with information about the incident is asked to contact the Vicksburg Police Department or submit an anonymous tip through Crime Stoppers at 601-355-TIPS (8477).

item.title: Suspect arrested in fatal shooting of 27-year-old in Jackson - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
JACKSON – Jackson police have arrested a suspect in connection with a fatal shooting that occurred early Monday morning.

According to the Jackson Police Department, homicide detectives identified 42-year-old Patrick Harris as the suspect in the shooting death of 27-year-old Anthony Watts.

Police said Watts was shot and killed earlier today in the 600 block of Northpointe Parkway.

Harris was arrested later that morning and charged with murder, aggravated assault and shooting into an occupied vehicle. He was booked into the Jackson Police Department holding facility and is awaiting his initial appearance in Municipal Court.

Jackson Police said the investigation remains ongoing.

Anyone with information about the case is asked to contact the Jackson Police Department at 601-960-1234 or Crime Stoppers at 601-355-TIPS (8477).

item.title: Six Wounded in Series of Tunica County Shootings; Multiple Suspects Arrested After High-Speed Chase - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Therese Apel
item.description:
TUNICA COUNTY — The Tunica County Sheriff’s Office is investigating a series of shootings that left at least six people wounded over two days in Tunica County and led to multiple arrests following a high-speed chase into DeSoto County.

Deputies first responded around 11:58 p.m. Saturday, March 7, after a 911 caller reported he had been shot in the area of Fitzgerald Boulevard in Robinsonville, according to the sheriff’s office. When deputies and first responders arrived, they found Jaylin Liddell, 18, of Tunica, suffering from a gunshot wound. Liddell was treated at the scene by Pafford Emergency Medical Services and transported to an area hospital.

Several hours later, at 3:08 a.m. Sunday, March 8, deputies were dispatched to the Tunica River Park at 1 Riverpark Drive after callers reported multiple people had been shot. At the scene, deputies located Recovyon Reed, 21, of Tunica, who had been shot. Reed was treated at the scene and transported to a hospital for treatment.

Authorities continued receiving reports of gunfire throughout the early morning hours. At 4:23 a.m., deputies responded to a report of shots fired in the parking lot of the Waffle House at 13132 U.S. Highway 61 in Robinsonville, but no victims were located.

Less than 30 minutes later, at 4:52 a.m., deputies were notified by a hospital that Deonte Taylor, 20, of Tunica, had arrived by personal vehicle seeking treatment for a gunshot wound.

At 5:18 a.m., deputies received another report of shots fired on Prichard Road in the Old Sub area of Tunica. Deputies in the area spotted a black Infiniti leaving the scene at a high rate of speed and attempted a traffic stop.

The vehicle failed to stop and led deputies on a high-speed chase into DeSoto County, traveling along Arkabutla Dam Road toward Arkabutla Lake Dam. The vehicle eventually crashed, and three occupants fled on foot. With assistance from the DeSoto County Sheriff’s Office, deputies pursued and apprehended the suspects.

Authorities identified the three individuals as Antonio Allen, 23, of Tunica; Mylon Done, 22, of Friars Point; and Darrius Robinson, 31, of Memphis. All three were taken into custody. During an inventory of the vehicle, deputies said they recovered multiple firearms in plain view.

At 5:23 a.m., deputies received another 911 call reporting a shooting at a residence on Gay Street in the Old Sub area of Tunica. Deputies found Ceedrick Wheeler, 21, of Tunica, suffering from a gunshot wound. Wheeler was treated at the scene and transported to a hospital.

Later, at 6:03 a.m., deputies were notified by another hospital that Khritavious Magsby, 30, of Clarksdale, had arrived seeking treatment for a gunshot wound.

Authorities said an additional suspect, Jon Talton “JT” Carter, 22, of Tunica, has also been taken into custody. Carter is charged with attempted murder in connection with the shooting that occurred at Tunica River Park.

The violence continued into the following day. At 1:16 p.m. Monday, March 9, deputies responded to reports of shots fired on Curtistine Drive in Tunica. Deputies found Mario Lester, 18, of Tunica, suffering from a gunshot wound. He was treated at the scene and transported to a hospital.

Investigators identified Charles Davis Jr., 19, of Tunica, as a person of interest in that shooting. Davis turned himself in to the Tunica County Sheriff’s Office at about 3:15 p.m. Monday and remained in custody as the investigation continued.

Sheriff’s officials said the investigation remains active and could lead to additional arrests. Authorities believe the shootings may be connected to gang activity and retaliatory violence.

The Tunica County Sheriff’s Office is being assisted by the Tunica Police Department, DeSoto County Sheriff’s Office, Mississippi Bureau of Investigation and the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives.

Anyone with information about the shootings is asked to contact the Tunica County Sheriff’s Office at (662) 363-1411 or Crime Stoppers at (662) 910-0400.

The following individuals are currently out on bond pending ongoing criminal investigations, according to the sheriff’s office:

• Jaylin Liddell – granted $50,000 bond for second-degree murder charge • Deonte Taylor – granted $25,000 bond for three capital murder charges, four counts of conspiracy, four counts of shooting into a motor vehicle, and one count of drive-by shooting • Ceedrick Wheeler – granted $25,000 bond for aggravated assault charge • Mario Lester – granted $100,000 bond for murder charge • Antonio Allen – granted $35,000 bond for conspiracy and accessory after the fact

item.title: Pearl police searching for missing juvenile last seen March 8 - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
PEARL – The Pearl Police Department is asking for the public’s help locating a missing juvenile.

Police said Kaili “Bug” Stapleton was last seen the afternoon of March 8 in the area of Boston Road and Payton Circle in Pearl.

Anyone who sees Stapleton or has information about her whereabouts is asked to contact the Pearl Police Department at 601-939-7000.

item.title: Oxford man charged after attempted sexual assault, kidnapping investigation - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
OXFORD – An Oxford man has been arrested after police investigated a reported attempted sexual assault that happened in late February, according to the Oxford Police Department.

Police said the department received a walk-in report March 5 regarding an attempted sexual assault that happened the night of Feb. 26.

During the investigation, officers learned the victim had been in the downtown area before leaving and walking toward her home. While walking along University Avenue, she was approached by a driver who offered her a ride home, police said.

After entering the vehicle, the driver took her to another location, where he allegedly held her against her will and attempted to sexually assault her.

Through investigative leads developed by the victim and investigators, police identified the suspect as Keilun Treveon Lewis, 19, of Oxford.

Lewis was arrested and charged with attempted rape and kidnapping. Bond was set by a Municipal Court judge.

Police said the case remains under investigation.

The Oxford Police Department is encouraging residents and visitors to stay alert and aware of their surroundings, particularly when accepting rides. Officials urged people not to enter vehicles with individuals they do not know and to verify rideshare details through the app before getting in.

Police also encouraged downtown visitors to use designated rideshare pickup locations near the north end of the Square by the parking garage, the west side behind the Lyric and the east side near Harrison’s and South 14th Street. Officials said those areas are well-lit and monitored by cameras.

Anyone with additional information about the incident, or who may have experienced a similar incident, is asked to contact the Oxford Police Department at 662-232-2400.

item.title: MISSING IN MISSISSIPPI: Stacy Hazelton, Bolivar County - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Therese Apel
item.description:
BOLIVAR COUNTY — More than two decades after she disappeared from a Bolivar County apartment complex, the remains of Stacy Hazelton have never been found, leaving her family without answers despite the conviction of the man accused of killing her.

Hazelton, a 21-year-old white female with brown hair and brown eyes, was last seen May 21, 2004, at the Alligator Place Apartments in Bolivar County. She stood about 5 feet 1 inch tall and weighed approximately 150 pounds at the time of her disappearance.

Investigators say Hazelton had moved to Mississippi earlier that year with her boyfriend, James Fannings Jr., but remained in close contact with family members in Pennsylvania. The couple lived with Fannings’ longtime friend, Chevaun Mack.

According to investigators, the couple soon began struggling financially. Authorities later learned Hazelton had told family members she wanted to return home and asked her parents to send money so she could leave Mississippi. Her parents sent the money, but Hazelton never returned.

After months without hearing from their daughter, Hazelton’s parents reported her missing in December 2004.

The case took a major turn in January 2006 when Fannings was arrested and charged with Hazelton’s murder. Investigators said Mack told authorities he witnessed Fannings shoot Hazelton in the head during an argument inside the apartment.

According to Mack’s account, Fannings forced him to help dispose of Hazelton’s body. The men placed her remains inside a barrel and loaded it into the back of Hazelton’s Chevrolet Blazer. Mack told investigators Fannings drove around with the barrel in the vehicle for several days before attempting to burn the body. The remains were then allegedly buried in a remote area near a creek in Duncan, Mississippi.

Authorities say Fannings later drove Hazelton’s Blazer to Philadelphia, Pennsylvania.

Fannings went to trial later in 2006. Prosecutors relied heavily on Mack’s testimony, and much of the remaining evidence was circumstantial. After about 16 minutes of deliberation, a jury found Fannings guilty of murder. He was sentenced to life in prison.

Fannings appealed the conviction in 2008, but the courts upheld the verdict.

Despite the conviction, Hazelton’s remains have never been located.

Authorities continue to urge anyone with information about the location of Hazelton’s remains to contact investigators.

Anyone with information can call the Pennsylvania State Police at (717) 299-7650, referencing case number J01-1049751, or the Bolivar County Sheriff’s Department at (662) 843-5378.

item.title: Men at SMCI graduate from trauma recovery program - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
LEAKESVILLE – Several incarcerated men at the South Mississippi Correctional Institution recently graduated from the Compassion Prison Project’s Trauma Talks program.

The graduation marked the completion of a 16-part curriculum aimed at addressing some of the underlying issues that can contribute to incarceration.

Program participants studied the effects of adverse childhood experiences, also known as ACEs, and learned tools to help regulate their nervous systems, challenge self-limiting beliefs and build empathy for themselves and others.

The Trauma Talks curriculum was founded by Fritzi Horstman and uses educational videos, interactive workbooks and peer-led group discussions to encourage accountability and vulnerability among participants.

Officials said the program offers an important opportunity for men at SMCI, which houses nearly 3,000 inmates, to break cycles of violence and prepare for successful reentry into their communities.

“When people realize their inherent value, they don’t just transform themselves; they create a ripple effect that makes the entire prison environment safer,” a Compassion Prison Project representative said.

The graduation is the latest example of the Mississippi Department of Corrections’ stated focus on rehabilitation and reentry efforts.

item.title: Man airlifted after assault outside Greenville club - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
GREENVILLE – The Greenville Police Department is investigating an assault that happened early Sunday morning outside a business on Highway 82 East.

Police said officers responded March 8 to the 1300 block of Highway 82 East, at Rack & Cue, after receiving a report of an assault.

When officers arrived, they spoke with a sheriff’s deputy who said the male victim had been escorted from the location earlier and was assaulted a short time later.

According to police, the victim said he was attacked in the parking lot by an unknown male after leaving the club.

The victim was taken to Delta Health for initial treatment and was later airlifted to another medical facility for further care.

Witnesses described the suspect as a Black male wearing a black shirt, blue jeans and a dark-colored sweater or hoodie.

Chief Marcus Turner said a video of the incident is circulating on social media and investigators are asking anyone who may recognize the suspect to help identify and locate the person of interest.

Police also said there were witnesses at the scene who have not yet come forward.

Anyone with information is asked to contact Crime Stoppers at 662-378-TIPS (8477) or submit a tip through the P3 app.

item.title: Jackson police work to identify mostly skeletal remains through tattoo - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Therese Apel
item.description:
JACKSON – Jackson police are asking for the public’s help in identifying a woman whose skeletal remains were discovered earlier this year in the city.

The remains were found March 7, 2025, near Greenwood Avenue in Jackson, according to the Jackson Police Department. Investigators later sent the remains to the Mississippi Crime Lab in Pearl for examination.

Authorities said the remains were determined to belong to a female believed to be white, though investigators said Hispanic ancestry could not be ruled out.

The woman is believed to have been between 26 and 46 years old and approximately 63.5 to 71.3 inches tall, according to the crime lab’s findings.

Police said the woman had tattoos, and images of those tattoos have been released in hopes someone may recognize them and help identify her. One of them may say, “10-18-20.” The other one is a hand-drawn heart with “July” written in cursive above it. Inside the heart is “2018.”

The case remains under investigation.

Anyone with information that could help identify the woman is asked to contact Crime Stoppers at 601-355-TIPS (8477) or the Jackson Police Department at 601-960-1234.

item.title: Jackson police investigate fatal shooting at apartment complex - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Therese Apel
item.description:
JACKSON — Jackson police are investigating a fatal shooting that left a 27-year-old man dead early Monday morning outside an apartment complex.

Officers with the Jackson Police Department responded around 12:09 a.m. March 9 to the 600 block of Northpointe Parkway, where they found the body of a Black male lying on the sidewalk between two apartment buildings, according to police.

The victim, identified as Anthony Watts, 27, had been shot multiple times and died from his injuries at the scene. Authorities said Watts’ next of kin have been notified.

Investigators believe the suspects were traveling in a late-model black Nissan occupied by three individuals. Police said the shooting is believed to stem from an ongoing dispute between the suspects and the victim, though the exact circumstances remain under investigation.

Detectives with the department’s Homicide Unit and Mobile Crime Unit responded to the scene, along with the Hinds County Coroner’s Office.

Watts’ death marks Jackson’s 14th homicide of the year, according to police.

The investigation is ongoing.

Anyone with information is asked to contact the Jackson Police Department at 601-960-1234 or Crime Stoppers at 601-355-8477.

item.title: Co-Lin awarded $18 million FEMA grant for safe room - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Special to Darkhorse Press
item.description:
WESSON – Copiah-Lincoln Community College has been awarded a major FEMA Hazard Mitigation Grant to construct a new, large-scale safe room facility. The $16,294,746 grant will be used for Phase II of construction on a nearly 35,000-square-foot standalone, multipurpose safe room on the Wesson Campus of Co-Lin.

An additional $175,000 administrative grant was awarded for the project, bringing the total FEMA investment in the project to just over $18 million, which includes a nearly $1.6 million planning and design grant awarded in 2024.

“We’ve been through enough severe storms in Mississippi to understand the value of safe rooms. I’m pleased with these FEMA hazard mitigation grants to create more safe spaces in our state,” said U.S. Senator Cindy Hyde-Smith, who serves on the Senate Homeland Security Appropriations Subcommittee that has jurisdiction over FEMA.

Co-Lin’s new multi-use facility will accommodate more than 2,000 occupants. In addition to serving as a safe room for the community, the facility will include a gymnasium to house the college’s basketball and volleyball programs.

“The safety of our students, employees, and community is always a top priority for Co-Lin,” said Co-Lin President Dr. Dewayne Middleton. “This project will provide a secure shelter during severe weather while also creating a first-class facility for our student-athletes and campus community. We are especially grateful to U.S. Senator Cindy Hyde-Smith for her continued leadership and strong support of Co-Lin, the community college system, and the people of Mississippi. Her commitment to strengthening our institutions and investing in projects like this makes a meaningful difference for our students and communities. We also extend our sincere appreciation to FEMA and our federal delegation for supporting a project that will serve our college and the surrounding community for generations. In addition, we greatly appreciate the Mississippi Emergency Management Agency for its assistance and support in helping make this important project a reality.”

item.title: Central MS Turtle Rescue seeking volunteers to keep Southaven facility open - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
SOUTHAVEN – Central MS Turtle Rescue is urgently seeking volunteers to help staff its northern facility in Southaven, warning that the location could close without additional help.

The rescue organization said it currently has no volunteers at the Southaven facility and needs at least one person who can commit to working two to three hours on a regular day each week.

Officials said the role requires a long-term commitment and is not intended for occasional volunteers or short-term summer help.

Volunteer duties include cleaning turtle enclosures and providing food and water for the animals. The work is done indoors and in air-conditioned conditions, according to the organization.

Central MS Turtle Rescue said having two or three volunteers would be ideal, but at least one regular volunteer is needed to keep the facility operating during the busy spring and summer months.

Anyone interested in volunteering is encouraged to contact the organization to learn more about the opportunity.

item.title: Bond set at $500K for Jackson man charged in alleged intentional fatal crash - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Therese Apel
item.description:
JACKSON — A Jackson man charged in what police say was an intentional crash that killed a woman and injured another cyclist was granted bond Monday during an initial appearance in municipal court.

Shannon Thomas, 57, received a $500,000 bond on a murder charge and a $10,000 bond for leaving the scene of an accident, according to the Jackson Police Department. He also faces an attempted murder charge in connection with the incident.

Police said the charges stem from a March 4 crash in the 4800 block of Chastain Drive involving two people riding a single bicycle.

Authorities said 21-year-old Kaitlyn Mangum-Gast and a 36-year-old man were traveling on the bicycle around 11:14 a.m. when a white pickup truck driven by Thomas struck them.

Mangum-Gast died from her injuries, and the case is now being investigated as Jackson’s 13th homicide of the year, police said.

Detectives initially responded to the incident as a crash but later determined during their investigation that the collision was intentional, according to police.

Thomas was arrested Friday, March 6, and booked into the Jackson Police Department holding facility. Investigators also towed his vehicle, which police say will be processed as evidence.

The investigation remains ongoing.

Anyone with information about the case is asked to contact the Jackson Police Department at 601-960-1234 or Crime Stoppers at 601-355-TIPS (8477).
item.ns1:encoded:
JACKSON — A Jackson man charged in what police say was an intentional crash that killed a woman and injured another cyclist was granted bond Monday during an initial appearance in municipal court.

Shannon Thomas, 57, received a $500,000 bond on a murder charge and a $10,000 bond for leaving the scene of an accident, according to the Jackson Police Department. He also faces an attempted murder charge in connection with the incident.

Police said the charges stem from a March 4 crash in the 4800 block of Chastain Drive involving two people riding a single bicycle.

Authorities said 21-year-old Kaitlyn Mangum-Gast and a 36-year-old man were traveling on the bicycle around 11:14 a.m. when a white pickup truck driven by Thomas struck them.

Mangum-Gast died from her injuries, and the case is now being investigated as Jackson’s 13th homicide of the year, police said.

Detectives initially responded to the incident as a crash but later determined during their investigation that the collision was intentional, according to police.

Thomas was arrested Friday, March 6, and booked into the Jackson Police Department holding facility. Investigators also towed his vehicle, which police say will be processed as evidence.

The investigation remains ongoing.

Anyone with information about the case is asked to contact the Jackson Police Department at 601-960-1234 or Crime Stoppers at 601-355-TIPS (8477).

item.title: Ag Commissioner Joins in Supporting Proposed Rule for the Federal Designation of Critical Habitats - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Special to Darkhorse Press
item.description:
JACKSON – Mississippi Commissioner of Agriculture and Commerce Andy Gipson joined more than 100 state and local officials representing 27 states in submitting a public comment in support of reforms to the federal procedures for designating critical habitats under the Endangered Species Act (ESA). The comment recommends the United States Department of the Interior and Fish and Wildlife Service finalize their proposed rule, “Endangered and Threatened Wildlife and Plants; Regulations for Designating Critical Habitat,” requiring more robust economic analysis before land is designated as a critical habitat.

“This proposed rule is a common-sense approach to the designation of critical habitats by ensuring federal agencies fully consider the real-world impacts on landowners and rural communities, and analyze if those costs outweigh the benefits,” said Gipson. “I am proud to join others in supporting this proposed rule to strengthen accountability and help ensure conservation efforts support environmental stewardship, as well as local and state economies.”

The proposed rule would amend portions of the Endangered Species Act of 1973 which provides a means to conserve the ecosystems upon which endangered and threatened species depend. Supporters say revisions would require consideration of the economic impact, the impact on national security and any other relevant impact of designating any particular area as critical habitat; and authorize the exclusion of areas from critical habitat if the benefits of excluding the area outweigh the benefits of designating it as critical habitat.

The public comment states, “We strongly support the Service’s proposed revisions to critical habitat designation procedures under the Endangered Species Act. These changes restore balance, accountability and respect for both species protection and the livelihoods of American families.”

Requiring stronger economic analysis would create a more transparent and balanced decision-making process while still protecting endangered species. In the public comment, officials state, “This deference to states encourages cooperative conservation, while protecting vulnerable species and the landowners who steward their land while making our economy strong.”

item.title: 18-year-old arrested in connection with Gulfport homicide - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
GULFPORT – An 18-year-old Gulfport man has been arrested and charged with homicide in connection with a deadly robbery investigation, according to the Gulfport Police Department.

Police said Ulrick Lamont Ellis was arrested March 8 and charged with one count of homicide.

The charge stems from an incident on Feb. 13, when officers responded at approximately 7:34 a.m. to a welfare check in the 3900 block of 21st Street.

When officers arrived, they found a man dead inside a vehicle. The victim was later identified as Rogeick Deandre Cohea III.

During the investigation, detectives identified Ellis as the suspect and determined that Cohea was killed during a robbery that went wrong, police said.

Authorities said Ellis was located March 8 with help from the Mississippi Bureau of Narcotics and Capitol Police. According to police, Ellis barricaded himself and was eventually forced out after officers deployed CS gas.

Anyone with information about the case is asked to contact the Gulfport Police Department at 228-868-5959 or Mississippi Coast Crime Stoppers at 877-787-5898. Anonymous tips may also be submitted online at mscrimestoppers.org.

item.title: Teen pedestrian dies after weekend crash in Monroe County - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
AMORY – A pedestrian injured in a Saturday crash in Monroe County has died, according to Monroe County Coroner Alan Gurley.

WTVA reported that Gurley identified the victim as Jake Harrison, 19, of Amory.

The crash happened on Boulevard Drive South near the intersection of Hatley Road.

According to WTVA, emergency crews responded to the scene and transported Harrison to a hospital in Amory. Due to the severity of his injuries, he was later transferred to a hospital in Jackson, where he died Sunday morning.

The Amory Police Department is continuing to investigate the cause of the crash.

item.title: Motorcyclist killed in crash on U.S. 98 in Greene County - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
GREENE COUNTY – A motorcyclist was killed Friday night in a crash on U.S. Highway 98 in Greene County, according to the Mississippi Highway Patrol.

The crash happened March 6 at approximately 9:45 p.m. near Mississippi Highway 57.

Authorities said a 2023 Harley-Davidson motorcycle driven by Thomas J. Everett, 69, of Daphne, Alabama, was traveling west on U.S. 98 when it left the roadway and struck a utility power pole.

Everett sustained fatal injuries and was pronounced dead at the scene by the Greene County Coroner’s Office.

The crash remains under investigation by the Mississippi Highway Patrol.

item.title: Jackson County Deputy injured in crash while responding to call - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
JACKSON COUNTY – A deputy was injured Friday afternoon in a crash involving a Jackson County Sheriff’s Department patrol vehicle, according to the Mississippi Highway Patrol.

The crash happened March 6 at approximately 3:37 p.m. on Old Fort Bayou Road.

Authorities said deputies with the Jackson County Sheriff’s Department were responding to a call for service with lights and sirens activated when one of the patrol vehicles collided with a Hyundai Santa Fe that was entering Old Fort Bayou Road from Rose Farm Road.

The deputy was transported to a local hospital with injuries. The driver of the Hyundai Santa Fe was not injured, officials said.

The crash remains under investigation by the Mississippi Highway Patrol.

item.title: DARKHORSE DOGS: Meet Maggie: Loyal, playful pup waiting years for a forever home - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
MADISON- After more than three years at the Webster Animal Shelter, Maggie is still waiting for the right person to take her home.

Maggie, a 5-year-old dog with a big personality and lots of love to give, has been at the shelter since March 2022. Shelter staff say she deserves a forever home after patiently waiting far longer than most dogs.

Maggie’s favorite things include soft squeaky toys, which she happily plays with and may even share with someone she trusts.

When it comes to people, Maggie tends to bond most closely with women, although she has connected with some men as well. Shelter staff say she would do best in a home without young children and ideally as the only pet.

However, Maggie has shown she can get along with certain male dogs with slow, proper introductions. Staff say she has formed bonds with a few of the male dogs at the shelter.

Maggie is an active dog who loves spending time outdoors. Despite being a little overweight at the moment, she enjoys running and would make a great walking or jogging companion.

Because she likes to explore, Maggie will need a secure environment. Shelter staff recommend a sturdy wooden or iron fence, a tall chain-link fence, or plenty of safe outdoor space where she can stretch her legs.

Maggie has been spayed, is heartworm negative and is up to date on all vaccinations, including rabies.

Her adoption fee is $100, and the shelter accepts cash or check.

Please go visit and give this sweet girl a chance. She has been waiting far too long for a forever home and she is so deserving!

Anyone interested in adopting Maggie or learning more can call the Webster Animal Shelter at 601-605-4729 or visit the shelter in person.

item.title: Woman riding bicycle hit by truck in Jackson - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
JACKSON – A woman riding a bicycle in Jackson was injured after she was hit by a white Dodge pick-up truck.

Thankfully, her injuries are minor, according to Jackson PIO Tommie Brown. The crash happened around 5:45 a.m. on Highway 18.

The driver remained on scene after the crash.

item.title: Tylertown police seek public’s help in hit-and-run investigation - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
TYLERTOWN – The Tylertown Police Department is asking for the public’s help identifying a woman in connection with a hit-and-run investigation.

Police said the department is currently investigating the incident and is seeking assistance in identifying the female pictured in a public notice.

Anyone who recognizes the woman or has information that could help investigators is asked to contact the Tylertown Police Department at 601-876-4440.

Officials said tipsters may also send the department a private message with the person’s name and remain anonymous.

item.title: Mississippi spring wild turkey season set to open March 14 - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
JACKSON – The Mississippi Department of Wildlife, Fisheries and Parks announced that the 2026 spring wild turkey season will open March 14 and run through May 3.

According to the department, the extension of the season follows state law, which allows the season to continue through the weekend when the closing date falls on a Friday.

Under Mississippi Code §49-7-31.2(c), when the open wild turkey season ends on a Friday, the season extends until 30 minutes after sunset on the following Sunday. Officials said the provision applies to this year’s season dates.

The extension does not increase or otherwise affect the annual bag limit for hunters.

Additional information about hunting seasons and bag limits can be found on the Mississippi Department of Wildlife, Fisheries and Parks website.

item.title: Mississippi Musicians Month celebrates state’s musical legacy - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Therese Apel
item.description:
MISSISSIPPI — Communities across the state will celebrate the third annual Mississippi Musicians Month this March with concerts, lectures, educational programs and special performances honoring the artists and traditions that helped shape American music.

The month long celebration highlights Mississippi’s influence on music genres ranging from Delta blues and gospel to jazz, country, rock, pop and classical music. Events will take place in communities across the state, recognizing musicians past and present and encouraging public participation through performances and cultural programming.

Mississippi Musicians Month was officially established in 2024 when Gov. Tate Reeves signed legislation designating March as the annual celebration. Organizers say the initiative was created to recognize Mississippi’s historic role in the global music landscape and encourage communities statewide to celebrate their local musical heritage.

Mississippi is often referred to as the “Birthplace of America’s Music,” producing influential artists whose work helped shape modern music. Early pioneers such as Charley Patton, Son House, Robert Johnson and Jimmie Rodgers helped define early blues and country traditions.

The state later produced internationally recognized performers including Elvis Presley, B.B. King, Muddy Waters, Jimmy Buffett, Faith Hill and opera star Leontyne Price. Mississippi’s impact also extends to songwriters such as Glen Ballard and Craig Wiseman, whose work has produced numerous chart-topping hits.

Organizers say the celebration is intended to include musicians and performances of all kinds, from singer-songwriters performing in coffee shops to blues festivals in the Delta and symphonic concerts.

“Great and timeless music is one of Mississippi’s chief exports,” officials with Opera Mississippi said. “By honoring the past while celebrating today’s artists and inspiring future generations, Mississippi Musicians Month affirms the state’s lasting role in shaping the soundtrack of American life.”

Founded in 1945, Opera Mississippi is the ninth oldest continuously operating opera company in the United States and the state’s only professional opera company.

item.title: Mississippi College adds two new online nursing Master’s programs - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Special to Darkhorse Press
item.description:
CLINTON – The Mississippi Institutions of Higher Learning has approved two new Master of Science in Nursing tracks for the School of Nursing at Mississippi College: one with a concentration in nursing education and the other with a concentration in nursing administration.

These options are fully online and join the school’s robust undergraduate and graduate offerings.

“The Mississippi College School of Nursing is expanding in significant ways,” said MC Nursing Dean Kelly Harden. “Applicants for these tracks aim to advance their education and leadership skills in today’s challenging healthcare environment.”

In addition, the school has also developed three fully online Doctor of Nursing (DNP) tracks that are currently in Stage II of IHL’s approval process.

The DNP in Nursing Education would be the first in the state, and the DNP in Executive Leadership would join only one other. Once approved by the IHL Board of Trustees, recruitment can begin.

Harden said nurses seeking admission to these tracks aim to become highly skilled educators and leaders who will improve healthcare, shape policy, lead change, and meet the needs of diverse populations in Mississippi and throughout the U.S.

“The addition of these MSN and DNP options to our portfolio is evidence of our commitment to our mission: ‘to cultivate mission-minded practitioners prepared to navigate complex healthcare systems with integrity, evidence-informed curiosity, and a commitment to continuous learning,’” she said.

MC Provost and Executive Vice President Mike Highfield said he is grateful to the IHL Board for acknowledging completion of Stage I in the approval process for the DNP program at MC.

“This is a milestone that reflects the strong work of our School of Nursing faculty and leadership in demonstrating both the clear workforce need and MC’s capacity to deliver a high-quality DNP program,” Highfield said. “While important steps remain, we are encouraged by the progress and remain committed to developing a program that will prepare advanced nursing leaders who will translate research into practice, improve patient outcomes, and strengthen healthcare education and systems across Mississippi.”

Remaining steps in Stage II of IHL’s approval process for the proposed DNP program include qualifying for the initial accreditation, the submission of a self-study for the DNP program, and hosting a site visit by the IHL’s nursing education staff.

item.title: Male Inmate Found Dead in Clarke County Jail, State Investigators Called In - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
A male inmate was found dead in his cell Friday at the Clarke County Jail, and state authorities have been called in to investigate.

Clarke County Sheriff Anthony Chancelor said the inmate was discovered around noon on March 6. The man was found in his cell by jail staff.

Chancelor said there were no apparent signs of foul play, and the death appears to be an apparent suicide.

The Mississippi Bureau of Investigation has been requested to take over and lead the investigation, which is standard procedure in deaths involving inmates.

Authorities have not released the inmate’s name. The investigation remains ongoing.
